title: Facility financing constraints and chronic disease continuity in rural Kenya
research_question: How do financing bottlenecks shape continuity of chronic care in rural primary facilities?
methods: Mixed-methods study using facility records, interviews, and process review across seven facilities.
main_findings: Facilities experienced frequent medicine stockouts and approval delays.
Restricted spending autonomy weakened continuity of hypertension and diabetes care.
operational_implications: Prioritize shorter procurement approval loops for chronic medicines.
Track reimbursement delays as a service delivery risk indicator.
equity_notes: Low-resource facilities carry the largest burden when financing is delayed or inflexible.
limitations: This output is a demonstration artifact and should be reviewed by a domain expert before use.
